articles.title: I was recently diagnosed with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis
articles.summary: Encephalitis is an autoimmune disorder where the body's normally helpful antibodies start acting strangely, leading to inflammation in the brain. This can cause symptoms such as flu-like symptoms, mental anxiety and panic attacks, physical problems like chronic jaw pain and balance issues, and even suicidal ideation and psychosis. The condition often goes undiagnosed or misdiagnosed, but getting diagnosed quickly is crucial for treatment, particularly with intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG) and methylprednisolone, which can help alleviate symptoms and prevent long-term damage.

articles.title: Hacking your PC using your speaker without ever touching it
articles.summary: Hacking your PC using your speaker without ever touching it is possible due to vulnerabilities in Creative Sound Blaster Katana V2X firmware. The device uses a proprietary protocol called CTP for communication over USB and requires challenge-response authentication before allowing commands to be executed, which can only be performed by static keys derived from the binaries that ship with the Creative App. Firmware analysis reveals that there are three parts to the firmware container: FBOOT, FMAIN, and CHK2, each containing significant value including a recovery mode for the speaker and a main firmware that is about 6.5 times larger than FBOOT.

articles.title: Fluid Simulation for Dummies
articles.summary: Fluid simulation for 3D graphics involves breaking down a complex fluid into a manageable number of boxes that interact with each other in real-time. This process is based on the Navier-Stokes equations, which describe how fluids behave under various forces and conditions. By simplifying these equations and using parallel processing techniques to simulate multiple interactions per second, developers can create realistic and immersive 3D fluid simulations for games and other applications.
